name: Gobi Aloo[cauliflower and Potatoes]
recipeIngredient: 1/2 large onion sliced thinly in long slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the potatoes in a saucepan and bring to boil. Boil for at least 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let them stand in water.
2. Cut the cauliflower into small bite-sized pieces (about 1 inch cubes), discarding most stem pieces.
3. Drain and rinse the cauliflower in a colander.
4. Heat oil in a wide skillet until very hot. Add mustard seeds and allow to pop.
5. Add bay leaves, cardamom pods, and cloves.
6. Stir for a while, then add onions. Cook until onions start to turn golden.
7. Add the rest of the spices (excluding turmeric) and stir.
8. Put the cauliflower in the skillet and fry with oil and spices for 2 minutes.
9. Cut potatoes into bite-sized pieces and add them to the skillet.
10. Add turmeric and stir. Continue stirring on medium heat for another couple of minutes.
11. Add half a cup of water, reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 5 minutes.
12. Check tenderness of vegetables. If too hard, add another 1/4 cup of water, cover, and cook for 5 more minutes.
13. Season with salt to taste and serve.
Source: Somesh Rao
